LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS MAINTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AS TO THE 3 RETALIATION CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DECLARATIONS OF THE DECISIONMAKERS (BROWNE DEC., PAR. 22; JEAN DEC., PAR.8; AND YAMAGUVCHI DEC., PAR. 3) SHOW THAT THE DISCHARGE WAS BASED ON LEGITIMATE PERFORMANCE ISSUES. IN OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUBSTANTIAL RESPONSIVE EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE PROFFERED BUSINESS REASON FOR THE DISCHARGE WAS PRETEXTUAL. SEE HERSANT V. DEPT. OF SOCIAL SERVICES, 57 CAL.APP.4TH 997. FIRST, BROWNE WAS ONE OF THE DECISION MAKERS AND HE CLEARLY HAD NOTICE OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINTS ABOUT DISCRIMINATORY TREATMENT. SECOND, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION SHOWS THAT BROWNE'S CONDUCT TOWARD PLAINTIFF CHANGED AFTER THE COMPLAINTS. THIRD, PLAINTIFF'S PRIOR UNBLEMISHED 20-YEAR WORK HISTORY AND POSITIVE EVALUATIONS IN 2009 AND 2010 (BROWNE COMPLETED THE 2010 EVALUATION) ARE PROBATIVE AND SHOW THAT DEFENDANT'S CLAIMED REASON FOR THE TERMINATION IS UNWORTHY OF CREDENCE. FOURTH, THE DECLARATIONS OF CO-WORKERS WILEY, TOWNE, AND TAMAYO SHOW THAT PROFANITY IS ROUTINELY USED AT THE WORKPLACE AND DEFENDANT MAY HAVE OVERBLOWN PLAINTIFF'S USE OF SUCH LANGUAGE AS A REASON FOR THE TERMINATION. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S REPLY ARGUMENT THAT MR. KENYON WAS THE PERSON THAT WAS REALLY RESPONSIBLE FOR THE DECISION TO TERMINATE PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT. DEFENDANT'S UNDISPUTED FACT NUMBER 22 AND PARAGRAPH 23 OF MR. BROWNE'S DECLARATION INDICATES THAT MR. BROWNE HAD A ROLE IN THE DECISIONMAKING PROCESS. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS MOOT AS TO THE REMAINING CLAIMS, WHICH WERE DISMISSED ON APRIL 7, 2015. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR NO. 5963 (302/EHG)
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-28-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-24-2015 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-28-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bianca N. Saad, of Bohm Law Group, Tel. 916.927.5574, for plaintiffs;
Elena R. Baca, of Paul Hastings LLP, Tel. 213.683.6306, for defendants.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is denied. Defendant has maintained its initial burden of production as to the 3 retaliation causes of action. The declarations of the decisionmakers (Browne dec., par. 22; Jean dec., par.8; and Yamaguvchi dec., par. 3) show that the discharge was based on legitimate performance issues. In opposition, Plaintiff has presented substantial responsive evidence creating a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the proffered business reason for the discharge was pretextual. See Hersant v. Dept. of Social Services, 57 Cal.App.4th 997. First, Browne was one of the decision makers and he clearly had notice of Plaintiff's complaints about discriminatory treatment. Second, Plaintiff's declaration shows that Browne's conduct toward Plaintiff changed after the complaints. Third, Plaintiff's prior unblemished 20-year work history and positive evaluations in 2009 and 2010 (Browne completed the 2010 evaluation) are probative and show that Defendant's claimed reason for the termination is unworthy of credence. Fourth, the declarations of co-workers Wiley, Towne, and Tamayo show that profanity is routinely used at the workplace and Defendant may have overblown Plaintiff's use of such language as a reason for the termination. The court rejects Defendant's reply argument that Mr. Kenyon was the person that was really responsible for the decision to terminate Plaintiff's employment. Defendant's undisputed fact number 22 and paragraph 23 of Mr. Browne's declaration indicates that Mr. Browne had a role in the decisionmaking process. The motion is denied as moot as to the remaining claims, which were dismissed on April 7, 2015. Prevailing party to prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Denise Doucette, CSR No. 5963; Dept. 302.
